mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth exceeding 150k jobs per month signals robust labor market demand, driving corporate earnings optimism and positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in periods of PAYEMS > 150k are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or 5d return volatility exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong aggregate demand, creating positive momentum in S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in periods of RSAFS/HOUST growth are negative or Rank IC < 0.05 across 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening supply chain capacity, prompting defensive sector rotation and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in INDPRO decline periods are positive or 20d return volatility < 1.8%.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 3% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank rate hikes, increasing equity discount rates and causing negative S&P 500 returns during monetary tightening regimes.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in CPI/FEDFUNDS tightening regimes are positive or Rank IC > 0.15 across 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% monthly rise in unemployment (UNRATE) combined with stagnant payroll growth (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in UNRATE/PAYEMS deterioration periods are positive or 5d return volatility < 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in GDP (GDPC1) indicates weakening economic demand, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in GDPC1 decline periods are positive or Rank IC > 0.10 across 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) amid falling unemployment (UNRATE) signals strong supply-side capacity and labor market tightness, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in INDPRO/UNRATE improvement periods are negative or Rank IC < 0.05 across 2-year windows.
